title: Valor & Villainy: Lludwig's Labyrinth - Detailed Unboxing
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7NbPwroPQ80

Daniel (@dungeondive) unboxes Valor & Villainy: Lludwig's Labyrinth, James Van Niekerk's bright, silly co-op crawl about chasing the villain Ludwig — who has kidnapped Giuseppe the caring Pizza Baron — through a world of eternal, uncaring darkness. There's a wrinkle he's rueful about: he backed the standee-only edition specifically to avoid minis and shrink the box, but the publisher scrapped that tier and generously upgraded all 214 of them to deluxe for free, leaving him with a mountain of plastic and the biggest square box in his collection. The game itself charms him — vibrant colours, a build-the-boss co-op mode, wood-punk dungeon tiles that pop out cleanly and form the table's border, choose-your-own-adventure event cards, and creative loot (floopy shrooms, a rusty sandwich, Bruno the horse). What sells him most is buried at the back of the campaign book: you can bypass the legacy campaign entirely, unlock everything at once, and play any mission in an infinitely repeatable quest mode — exactly what a lapsed campaigner wants. He praises the thoughtful legacy touches, like envelope icons printed on the punchboard so components return to their right home for a reset.

----

Valor & Villainy lets you skip the legacy campaign and unlock everything for infinite one-off quests. Is a built-in 'just let me play' bypass a must-have for you, or does it undercut the point of a campaign?
